mission.title: operator-framework: quickstart does not work
mission.description: quickstart does not work. This issue affects 20+ users.

steps.title: Identify operator-framework troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your operator-framework deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework -n operator-framework --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference [recovered]`

steps.title: Review operator-framework configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant operator-framework configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get configmap -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=operator-framework
```
## Bug Report



#### What did you do?



I followed the quickstart documentation found at https://v1-33-x.sdk.operatorframework.io/docs/building-operators/golang/quickstart/

> ./trysdk.sh &> trysdk.log

```bash
#/bin/bash

set -o

steps.title: Apply the fix for quickstart does not work
steps.description:
For **MAC darwin/arm64** users following will work:

1. Your go version should be 1.27
```bash
go version go1.21.7 darwin/arm64
```

2. No need to pass `--plugins=go/v4-alpha` argument because [kubebuilder documentation](https://kubebuilder.io/plugins/available-plugins) has made
```yaml
#/bin/bash

set -o xtrace

operator-sdk version

cat /etc/os-release

go version

# EVERYTHING HERE IS COPIED *VERBATIM* FOR THE GO QUICKSTART LOCATED AT
# https://v1-33-x.sdk.operatorframework.io/docs/building-operators/golang/quickstart/

mkdir memcached-operator

cd memcached-operator

operator-sdk init --domain example.com --repo github.com/example/memcached-operator

operator-sdk create api --group cache --version v1alpha1 --kind Memcached --resource --controller

make docker-build docker-push IMG="example.com/memcached-operator:v0.0.1"
```

steps.title: Upgrade operator-framework to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade operator-framework:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade operator-framework operator-framework/operator-framework --namespace operator-framework
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework
helm list -n operator-framework
```

steps.title: Confirm quickstart does not work is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework -n operator-framework --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n operator-framework --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference [recovered]` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
For **MAC darwin/arm64** users following will work:

1. Your go version should be 1.27
```bash
go version go1.21.7 darwin/arm64
```

2.
codeSnippets:
#/bin/bash

set -o xtrace

operator-sdk version

cat /etc/os-release

go version

# EVERYTHING HERE IS COPIED *VERBATIM* FOR THE GO QUICKSTART LOCATED AT
# https://v1-33-x.sdk.operatorframework.io/docs/building-operators/golang/quickstart/

mkdir memcached-operator

cd memcached-operator

operator-sdk init --domain example.com --repo github.com/example/memcached-operator

operator-sdk create api --group cache --version v1alpha1 --kind Memcached --resource --controller

make docker-build docker-push IMG="example.com/memcached-operator:v0.0.1"

$ go version               
go version go1.21.9 darwin/arm64

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with operator-framework installed or the issue environment reproducible.
